_note: Conviction theme CANDIDATE WATCHLIST. As of T2 (council.enabled=true) this is the candidate set the LLM council JUDGES — it ranks inflection / structural-vs-fad / cleanest-name / bull-vs-bear and PROPOSES which to trade (it does NOT discover new themes; that's T3). With council.enabled=false the paper loop consumes these directly (the T1 hand-seeded loop). Each: name, symbol (cleanest single-name expression), direction ('bullish'=tailwind→calls, 'bearish'=rollover→puts), thesis, active. The deterministic IV/cheap-convexity gate (PREREG_THEMATIC_CONVEXITY §4) decides whether each is actually traded — a beloved, richly-priced theme is vetoed however high the council's conviction. Run offline: `python orchestrator.py --demo`.

themes.name: copper_electrification
themes.thesis: EXAMPLE — replace. Electrification + grid/datacenter buildout copper demand against constrained mine supply; hypothesis is an unloved industrial tailwind whose long-dated call IV has not yet priced the structural deficit ('copper-not-rockets'). DEACTIVATED 2026-07-02 (knob-0): a placeholder consuming a protected judged slot every cycle buys nothing; FCX still reaches the union as a sentinel whenever discovery surfaces it, and the open FCX shadow position is unaffected (positions are independent of themes).

themes.name: gate_rich_canary
themes.thesis: CANARY, not a thesis (relabeled 2026-07-02, knob-0; was EXAMPLE_rich_theme_delete_me). A standing gate-rich name that predictably structures on the real chain and reaches council deliberation guarantees one full-path roundtrip per L1 — the deterministic discriminator between an inert apparatus and a genuinely all-NEUTRAL cycle (the #37 ambiguity class). It is EXPECTED to be IV-gate-vetoed every cycle (iv_rv ~1.2+); were the council ever to include it, the proposal faces the gates like any other. The --demo synthetic gives this symbol rich IV so the veto path is always exercised offline too.
